verdict: Sweetgreen is genuinely easy to eat healthy at if you anchor on a lean protein and a greens base; the trap is the calorie-dense composed bowls and the 14g-fat dressings. Anchor strategy: any salad + Blackened Chicken with dressing on the side.

lose_weight.name: Kale Caesar with Roasted Chicken
lose_weight.why: 35g protein for only 405 cal and 13g carbs — the best protein-to-calorie ratio on the menu.

lose_weight.name: Hummus Crunch Salad
lose_weight.why: Lowest-calorie real bowl on the menu; add a lean protein to lift it without much extra fat.

maintain.name: Chicken Pesto Parm
maintain.why: Balanced 37g protein and 48g carbs with controlled fat — the best all-around composed bowl.

maintain.name: Super Green Goddess
maintain.why: Veg-forward with 15g fiber, keeps you full at under 500 cal.

build_muscle.name: Hot Honey Chicken Plate
build_muscle.why: Highest protein on the entire menu — 54g in one plate.

build_muscle.name: Chicken + Avocado + Ranch Plate
build_muscle.why: 50g protein with healthy avocado fats for a clean lean-bulk meal.

order_hacks: Add Blackened Chicken (+140 cal, +21g protein, only 6g fat) — the leanest protein boost on the menu.
Get dressing on the side; Balsamic Vinaigrette is 150 cal / 14g fat, so a half-pour saves ~75 cal.
Sub Warm Quinoa or Shredded Kale for the wild-rice base to cut sugar and carbs without losing volume.
Skip the add-on Avocado (160 cal) when cutting and use Roasted Almonds (80 cal) for crunch instead.

treat.name: Crispy Rice Bowl
treat.why: Crispy-rice texture and sauce scratch the takeout itch while still landing 31g protein and staying under the Harvest Bowl in fat.

verdict: CAVA is one of the easiest places to eat genuinely healthy because the proteins are lean and you control every gram; the only landmines are falafel, full-fat dressings, and stacking three or more dips. Anchor strategy: SuperGreens base + a double lean protein, then spend remaining calories on one grain OR two dips.

lose_weight.name: SuperGreens + Grilled Chicken + Tzatziki
lose_weight.why: Leanest real meal at CAVA — 33g protein on greens with no grain for ~325 cal.

lose_weight.name: SuperGreens + Grilled Chicken + Roasted Veg + Tzatziki
lose_weight.why: Veg and lean chicken on greens, no grain — filling and fiber-rich under 450.

maintain.name: Tahini Caesar Bowl
maintain.why: Official composed bowl — balanced 37g protein on a greens base.

maintain.name: SuperGreens + Harissa Honey Chicken + Brown Rice + Hummus
maintain.why: Balanced grain bowl built on the fan-favorite sweet-spicy chicken.

build_muscle.name: Chicken + RightRice Bowl
build_muscle.why: Official bowl — 46g protein, and RightRice doubles as a high-protein grain.

build_muscle.name: Black Lentils + Double Grilled Chicken + SuperGreens + Tzatziki
build_muscle.why: Roughly 80g protein — the highest-protein build at CAVA, with lentils adding 17g.

order_hacks: Double the protein — chicken is 250 cal / 28g and it's the biggest single upgrade to any bowl's macros.
Base on SuperGreens (40 cal) instead of rice (290-370 cal) to bank 250+ cal for protein and dips.
Black Lentils is the smartest 'grain' — 17g protein and 15g fiber for 260 cal vs basmati's 6g protein.
Skip Greek Vinaigrette (14g fat); use Yogurt Dill or Lemon-Herb Tahini and add a spread for ~35-45 cal.

treat.name: Spicy Chicken + Avocado Pita
treat.why: The warm pita with spicy chicken and avocado is the most crave-worthy thing on the menu, and at least brings 50g protein to the splurge.

verdict: Qdoba is very controllable and uniquely lets you stack free guac plus multiple free salsas while subbing in cauliflower or lettuce; the only real traps are the tortilla, queso, and double rice. Anchor strategy: double grilled chicken on cauliflower-mash or lettuce, load free veggies, salsa and guac, skip the tortilla and queso.

lose_weight.name: Keto Bowl - Chicken
lose_weight.why: Official low-carb bowl — 33g protein and only 11g carbs on a cauliflower base.

lose_weight.name: Chicken + Black Beans + Fajita Veggies + Pico + Salsa Verde (no rice)
lose_weight.why: Double-fiber and lean — 38g protein under 360 cal with no rice.

maintain.name: Chicken Bowl: Cilantro Lime Rice + Black Beans + Pico + Guac
maintain.why: The classic balanced burrito bowl with free guac.

maintain.name: Citrus Lime Chicken Salad
maintain.why: Official composed salad — 35g protein on a greens base.

build_muscle.name: Double Protein Bowl - Chicken
build_muscle.why: Highest protein on the menu — 59g in one official bowl.

build_muscle.name: Chicken + Brown Rice + Black Beans + Shredded Cheese + Pico
build_muscle.why: 43g protein with brown rice and beans for a clean muscle-build bowl.

order_hacks: Get a bowl, not a burrito — the 12.5" flour tortilla alone is 300 cal / 7g fat / 52g carbs.
Swap rice for Cauliflower Mash or lettuce to cut ~150 cal and ~37g carbs while keeping the bowl full.
Guac is free on entrees — take it; the queso and sour-cream combo adds more fat for less benefit.
Double the chicken: each 3.5oz scoop is +170 cal and +23g protein with only 9g fat.

treat.name: Chicken Queso Bowl
treat.why: Loaded queso indulgence that still delivers 47g protein, making it the least-damaging 'loaded' order.

verdict: El Pollo Loco is one of the easiest fast-food chains to eat healthy at because flame-grilled chicken IS the identity. Anchor strategy: make the skinless grilled breast (180 cal / 35g protein / 0 carb) your base, add black beans and fresh veggies, and dial back rice, cheese, sour cream and creamy dressings.

lose_weight.name: Double Chicken Avocado Salad (no dressing)
lose_weight.why: Best protein-per-calorie on the menu; only 14g carbs without the dressing.

lose_weight.name: Chicken Tostada Salad (no shell, no dressing)
lose_weight.why: Big, filling chicken-and-veggie salad once you skip the fried shell and creamy dressing.

maintain.name: Chicken Black Bean Bowl (Pollo Fit)
maintain.why: Balanced one-bowl meal with only 11g fat and high fiber.

maintain.name: Original Pollo Bowl
maintain.why: Classic rice-beans-chicken bowl that keeps you full at moderate calories.

build_muscle.name: 3pc Flame-Grilled Chicken (skinless, a la carte)
build_muscle.why: Pure protein anchor — 60g+ protein and near-zero carbs; pull the skin to cut fat.

build_muscle.name: Double Chicken Avocado Salad (no dressing)
build_muscle.why: 48g protein for 370 cal — the best muscle ratio on the menu.

smart_swaps.instead_of: Classic Tostada Salad, fried shell + dressing (830 cal)
smart_swaps.get: Tostada Salad, no shell, no dressing (430 cal)

smart_swaps.instead_of: Double Chicken Avocado Salad WITH dressing (530 cal)

order_hacks: Order chicken a la carte and ask for it skinless — drops the breast from ~220 to 180 cal, keeps all 35g protein.
On any salad, ask for no fried shell and dressing on the side — that's where 300-400 cal and most of the fat hide.
Default to black beans over pinto (140 cal / 8g protein vs 200 / 11g) — saves ~60 cal and is nearly fat-free.
Build a Pollo Fit bowl: double chicken + black beans + fresh veggies, light or no rice, skip sour cream and cheese.

treat.name: Original Pollo Bowl
treat.why: The crave-worthy rice-beans-cheese-salsa comfort bowl that's still grilled-chicken-based and only 12g fat — the most satisfying splurge that won't blow the day.

verdict: Tim Hortons is easy to keep healthy if you respect the cup — the coffee is fine; the cream, sugar and pastries are the damage. Anchor strategy: black or Americano coffee (or a small skim latte) plus an egg-and-cheese English muffin keeps you at 270-440 cal with real protein before any pastry tempts you.

lose_weight.name: Plain Homestyle Oatmeal (small) + black coffee
lose_weight.why: High-fiber, near-zero-fat warm breakfast; black coffee adds 0-5 cal.

lose_weight.name: Egg & Cheese English Muffin
lose_weight.why: Lowest-cal real egg breakfast sandwich with solid protein on a portion-controlled muffin.

maintain.name: Bacon, Egg & Cheese English Muffin
maintain.why: Balanced classic; bacon adds flavor and protein without going overboard.

maintain.name: Greek Yogurt w/ Berries & Granola + small 2% Latte
maintain.why: Protein-and-fruit combo that eats like a full breakfast.

build_muscle.name: Egg & Cheese English Muffin + small Protein Latte
build_muscle.why: About 33g protein under 450 cal — the chain's best high-protein combo.

build_muscle.name: Bacon & Cheese Omelette Bites (2pc) + small Protein Latte
build_muscle.why: Highest protein-per-calorie play and low carb.

smart_swaps.get: Bacon Egg & Cheese English Muffin (330 cal)
Coffee with a splash of milk, no sugar (~25 cal)

smart_swaps.instead_of: Boston Cream Donut as breakfast (230 cal)
smart_swaps.get: Greek Yogurt with berries & granola (250 cal)

order_hacks: Black coffee or Americano is ~0-5 cal; a medium Double-Double is 200+ cal — the cream and sugar are the problem, not the coffee.
Order lattes small with skim or 2% milk; the milk is your protein (9-17g) — skip flavor syrups (each pump is ~50 cal).
Choose an English muffin base over a bagel, biscuit, or croissant — saves ~120-200 cal and a lot of fat.
Pick egg-white or omelette-bite options for protein with the least fat, and ask for no hash brown in combos.

treat.name: Boston Cream Donut
treat.why: The iconic custard-filled, chocolate-topped classic — one of the lower-fat donuts and far lighter than a filled-and-frosted Dream donut or an Iced Capp.

verdict: Smoothie King is a sugar trap disguised as health food — many 'fitness' blends top 600-1000 cal with 50g+ sugar at large sizes. Anchor strategy: order the smallest 20oz size, build on the Gladiator line for muscle (45-46g protein, ~1g sugar) or Slim-N-Trim for weight loss, and cut added sweeteners.

lose_weight.name: Slim-N-Trim Vanilla (20oz)
lose_weight.why: Lightest real smoothie; 14g protein, low fat, stevia-sweetened.

lose_weight.name: Slim-N-Trim Veggie (20oz)
lose_weight.why: Veggie-blend Slim option with high protein-to-calorie and filling fiber.

maintain.name: Slim-N-Trim Veggie (20oz)
maintain.why: Balanced and light enough to leave room in the day.

maintain.name: Gladiator Chocolate + half banana add-in (20oz)
maintain.why: Builds the lean Gladiator base into a fuller, balanced meal.

build_muscle.name: Gladiator Chocolate (20oz)
build_muscle.why: 46g protein and 1g sugar — the cleanest muscle smoothie on the menu.

build_muscle.name: Gladiator Vanilla (20oz)
build_muscle.why: Same lean high-protein base, unsweetened and endlessly customizable.

order_hacks: Always order the 20oz — Smoothie King defaults to big sizes; 32/40oz can nearly double calories AND sugar.
Ask them to 'make it skinny' and hold the added turbinado sugar or honey many blends include.
Swap to almond milk or a water base to cut calories and saturated fat vs juice or sherbet bases.
Start from the Gladiator unsweetened high-protein base and add half a banana or berries instead of a sugar-loaded blend.

treat.name: Peanut Power Plus (20oz)
treat.why: Rich peanut-butter-banana shake that tastes like dessert but still delivers 20g protein and zero added sugar — just order the 20oz, not the 40.

verdict: You can eat reasonably at Cane's because the fingers themselves are a solid 130 cal / 13g protein each — the damage is entirely in the sauce, toast, fries, and lemonade. Anchor strategy: plain fingers a la carte plus water or unsweet tea turns a 1,250-cal combo into a 400-520 cal protein plate.

lose_weight.name: 2 Chicken Fingers + Coleslaw, no sauce
lose_weight.why: Smallest realistic combo; slaw adds bulk for far fewer calories than fries.

lose_weight.name: 3 Chicken Fingers, no sauce
lose_weight.why: Pure protein anchor — fingers only, sauce skipped saves ~190 cal of fat.

maintain.name: 4 Chicken Fingers, no sauce
maintain.why: A full Box's worth of protein without the fries, toast and sauce that double the calories.

maintain.name: 3 Chicken Fingers + Coleslaw + 1 dipping sauce
maintain.why: Balanced ~600-cal plate; one small sauce instead of the full cup keeps fat in check.

build_muscle.name: 6 Chicken Fingers (Caniac), no sauce/fries
build_muscle.why: Highest-protein order on the menu — strip the combo sides and it's nearly 80g protein.

build_muscle.name: 4 Chicken Fingers + 1 sauce + Texas Toast
build_muscle.why: High protein plus carbs from toast to refuel after training; skip the fries.

smart_swaps.get: 4 Chicken Fingers a la carte (520 cal)
Skip it or dip light in BBQ (~45 cal)

order_hacks: Order fingers a la carte (or a Caniac but bag the fries) — the combo's fries, toast and sauce add ~700 cal.
Skip the Cane's Sauce: -190 cal and -19g fat for the cup, the single biggest hidden fat bomb here.
Ditch the Texas toast (-140 cal, butter-soaked).
Swap fries for coleslaw (-290 cal) and get unsweet tea or water, not lemonade (-180 cal).

treat.name: The Sandwich (3 fingers, sauce, bun)
treat.why: The crave-worthy Cane's experience, sauce and all, but it still delivers 48g protein — far better than a Box Combo for the same indulgence.

verdict: Zaxby's is one of the easier fried-chicken stops to eat clean because the grilled Zalad and grilled sandwich are genuinely lean — the whole game is grilled-not-fried and Lite Vinaigrette-not-Ranch. Anchor strategy: a Grilled House Zalad with dressing on the side lands at ~400 cal and 46g protein.

lose_weight.name: Garden House Zalad (no chicken) + Lite Vinaigrette
lose_weight.why: Lightest real meal; add grilled chicken for ~150 cal more if you want more protein.

lose_weight.name: Grilled House Zalad, no toast, Lite Vinaigrette on side
lose_weight.why: Grilled chicken over greens with the 30-cal vinaigrette — highest protein-per-calorie on the menu.

maintain.name: Grilled Chicken Sandwich (no fries)
maintain.why: One handheld, balanced at ~470 cal with 38g protein when you skip the combo fries and drink.

maintain.name: Grilled House Zalad with toast + Lite Vinaigrette
maintain.why: Full salad with the toast left on, still under 600 cal when you avoid ranch.

build_muscle.name: Grilled Zalad + extra grilled chicken, Lite Vinaigrette
build_muscle.why: About 60g protein and low carb — double the grilled fillet and keep the dressing light.

build_muscle.name: 5 Chicken Fingerz, not sauced (no fries/toast)
build_muscle.why: 46g protein in one order; fried but the highest-protein non-salad option if you skip the sides.

smart_swaps.get: Grilled Chicken Sandwich, no fries (470 cal)

order_hacks: Get GRILLED — the grilled sandwich (470 cal) and grilled Zalad beat anything fried by 200-400 cal.
Ask for dressing on the side and pick Lite Vinaigrette (30 cal) over Ranch (190-200 cal) — the biggest single save on a salad.
Drop the Texas toast that comes on Zalads and plates (-~150 cal).
Go light on Zax Sauce (180 cal / 18g fat) and sub a side salad for crinkle fries.

treat.name: 5 Chicken Fingerz with Zax Sauce
treat.why: The signature Zaxby's fix — hand-breaded fingerz with Zax Sauce — and it still packs 47g protein, so it's the least-damaging way to indulge if you bag the fries and toast.

verdict: Bojangles is very doable healthy because the Grilled Chicken Salad (360 cal / 40g protein) and grilled sandwich are legitimately lean, and the green beans and Cajun pintos are some of the best low-cal sides in fast food. Anchor strategy: grilled chicken + a bean or veg side, and save the Bo-Berry biscuit for a true cheat day.

lose_weight.name: 3 Chicken Supremes + Green Beans
lose_weight.why: Fewest tenders plus the near-zero-cal veg side instead of a biscuit or fries.

lose_weight.name: Grilled Chicken Salad, light dressing on side
lose_weight.why: 40g protein and only 9g carbs — the leanest high-protein meal in the building.

maintain.name: Grilled Chicken Sandwich (no mayo)
maintain.why: Balanced handheld at ~470 cal; ask for no mayo to shave fat further.

maintain.name: 4 Chicken Supremes + Cajun Pintos
maintain.why: Protein-forward plate where the pintos (110 cal, 7g protein) replace the biscuit or fries.

build_muscle.name: Grilled Chicken Salad + 3 Chicken Supremes
build_muscle.why: Stacks the 40g-protein salad with supremes for ~63g protein, still low-ish carb.

build_muscle.name: 4 Chicken Supremes + Cajun Pintos + Green Beans
build_muscle.why: 40g protein with two fiber-rich bean and veg sides instead of one starchy biscuit.

order_hacks: Go grilled, not fried — the Grilled Chicken Sandwich (470 cal) and Grilled Chicken Salad (360 cal) beat the fried filet by 150-200+ cal.
Pick a lean side: Green Beans (~25 cal) or Cajun Pintos (110 cal) over Dirty Rice (170), Mac & Cheese, or fries.
Skip the Bo-Berry Biscuit as a 'side' — it's a 370-cal dessert (18g sugar, 17g fat) hiding in plain sight.
Get salad dressing on the side and use half; ask for no mayo on grilled sandwiches.

treat.name: Bo-Berry Biscuit
treat.why: The iconic blueberry-studded, glazed Bojangles crave — a genuine dessert, but at 370 cal it's the most reasonable single splurge if you anchor the meal on grilled chicken.

verdict: Wingstop is one of the easier indulgent chains to eat well at because classic wings are essentially pure protein at 0g carbs — the calories live almost entirely in the fries, the dips and the creamy sauces. Anchor strategy: 6-10 classic bone-in wings with a dry rub or thin wet sauce, no fries, no ranch.

lose_weight.name: 6 Classic Plain Wings (no fries, no dip)
lose_weight.why: Pure protein, zero carbs — the leanest real order once you skip the fries and ranch.

lose_weight.name: 8 Original Hot Classic Wings (no sides)
lose_weight.why: Original Hot is a thin wet sauce, so you get heat and flavor with barely any added calories over plain.

maintain.name: 10 Classic Plain Wings + celery (no dip)
maintain.why: Big satisfying portion around 510 cal with a massive 100g protein hit; celery adds crunch for almost nothing.

maintain.name: 6 Lemon Pepper Classic Wings + carrots
maintain.why: Lemon Pepper is a dry rub (1g carb) so it stays balanced, paired with a veg side instead of fries.

build_muscle.name: 10 Classic Plain Wings
build_muscle.why: 100g protein and zero carbs — about as protein-dense as fast food gets.

build_muscle.name: 10 Original Hot Classic Wings
build_muscle.why: Three-quarters of your day's protein in one order with a wet sauce that adds almost no calories.

smart_swaps.get: Wings + extra dry rub or celery (~0 cal)

smart_swaps.instead_of: Garlic Parmesan wings + Seasoned Fries (+390 cal)

order_hacks: Order CLASSIC bone-in, not boneless — boneless are breaded nuggets (more carbs, less protein); classic are real wings at 0g carbs.
Pick dry rubs or thin wet sauces (Lemon Pepper, Original Hot, Louisiana Rub) over creamy ones to add ~0g carb vs ranch or parm.
Skip the dips or use the rub for flavor — one Ranch cup (313 cal, 33g fat) can out-calorie six plain wings.
Swap fries for veggie sticks: carrots and celery are 5-17 cal vs ~390 cal for regular seasoned fries.

treat.name: 8 Garlic Parmesan Classic Wings
treat.why: Rich, buttery, cheesy and crave-worthy — but still 64g protein and only 4g carbs, so it's the indulgent pick that does the least damage to your macros.

verdict: Shake Shack is calorie-dense but workable: the patties are quality protein and a single burger fits almost any goal — the trouble is the fries-plus-shake combo. Anchor strategy: a single burger (or grilled chicken), skip or split the shake, and treat fries as a shared side.

lose_weight.name: Single Hamburger (no cheese)
lose_weight.why: The lowest-calorie real burger on the menu — one Angus patty on a potato bun with 25g protein.

lose_weight.name: Single Cheeseburger
lose_weight.why: Adds cheese and 4g protein for only 70 more calories — still a sensible single under 500.

maintain.name: Single ShackBurger
maintain.why: The classic with ShackSauce and lettuce/tomato lands right in the 500-cal sweet spot with 29g protein.

maintain.name: Single Cheeseburger + split a small fry
maintain.why: Keep the burger as the anchor and share fries so you stay near 600 instead of solo-ing a 470-cal fry.

build_muscle.name: Double ShackBurger
build_muscle.why: Two patties deliver 51g protein in one sandwich — the highest-protein single item on the menu.

build_muscle.name: Chick'n Shack (grilled if available)
build_muscle.why: 33g protein from a chicken breast; ask for it grilled where offered to cut the fry-batter fat.

smart_swaps.get: ShackBurger only, water or diet soda

order_hacks: Order a SINGLE, not a double — a single ShackBurger (500) vs double (790) is the biggest lever on the menu.
Ask for it lettuce-wrapped / no bun to drop ~24g carbs and ~120 cal while keeping all the protein.
Skip or SPLIT the shake — a Vanilla Shake (680 cal) can have more calories than the burger itself.
Choose plain crinkle fries over cheese fries (470 vs 710), or share one order across the table.

treat.name: Vanilla Hand-Spun Shake
treat.why: A real frozen-custard shake — thick, rich and the thing Shake Shack is famous for; 18g protein softens the blow, and splitting it halves the damage to ~340 cal.

verdict: DQ is two restaurants in one: the grilled chicken sandwich (390 cal, 29g protein) is a genuinely solid pick, while the Blizzards are dessert-as-a-meal calorie bombs. Anchor strategy: grilled chicken or a single cheeseburger, and if you want the famous Blizzard, order it MINI.

lose_weight.name: Grilled Chicken Sandwich (no mayo)
lose_weight.why: Best protein-to-calorie ratio on the savory menu — 29g protein under 400 cal; no mayo trims more fat.

lose_weight.name: Small Vanilla Cone
lose_weight.why: The lowest-calorie way to actually get DQ soft-serve — a portion-controlled cone instead of a 600+ cal Blizzard.

maintain.name: Original Cheeseburger
maintain.why: A single classic cheeseburger is a balanced ~400-cal anchor with 22g protein.

maintain.name: Grilled Chicken Sandwich + Side Salad
maintain.why: Adds volume and greens to the leanest sandwich while staying in the maintenance band.

build_muscle.name: 4-Piece Chicken Strip Basket
build_muscle.why: Highest single-order protein (35g) plus carbs to refuel — share or skip the toast and gravy to cut it down.

build_muscle.name: Grilled Chicken Sandwich + Original Cheeseburger
build_muscle.why: Stacking the two leanest mains gets you 51g protein from real meat without the deep fryer.

smart_swaps.get: Mini Choc Chip Cookie Dough Blizzard (420 cal)

order_hacks: Get the GRILLED Chicken Sandwich, never the crispy/breaded — grilled saves roughly 150-250 cal and a load of fat.
Size DOWN on frozen treats: a MINI Blizzard (420) vs a medium (700-1000+) is the single biggest save at DQ.
A small Vanilla Cone (220) or a No-Sugar-Added Fudge Bar (50 cal, 4g protein) scratches the ice-cream itch for a fraction of a Blizzard.
Skip the fries-and-toast that pad the Chicken Strip Basket to 1020 cal — order strips a la carte for the protein only.

treat.name: Mini Chocolate Chip Cookie Dough Blizzard
treat.why: The iconic DQ Blizzard — cookie dough chunks in soft-serve — in the mini size, so you get the full crave-worthy experience at ~420 cal instead of 700-1000+ for a medium.
